user.description: Set required parameters to connect to your router

data_description.username: This is always 'root' by default on GL-iNet devices
data_description.host: The hostname or IP address of your GL-iNet router.
data_description.password: The password you use to login to your router's admin page, this is unlikely to be the same as your WiFi password
data_description.consider_home: Seconds to wait before deciding that a device is away

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate with your GL-iNet router
reauth_confirm.description: The stored credentials for your GL-iNet router were rejected. Enter the current password to reconnect.

data_description.username: This is always 'root' by default on GL-iNet devices
data_description.password: The password you use to login to your router's admin page, this is unlikely to be the same as your WiFi password

data_description.username: This is always 'root' by default on GL-iNet devices
data_description.host: The hostname or IP address of your GL-iNet router.
data_description.password: The password you use to login to your router's admin page, this is unlikely to be the same as your WiFi password
data_description.consider_home: Seconds to wait before deciding that a device is away

statistics_not_collecting.title: Flow statistics are not collecting per-app data
statistics_not_collecting.description:
Flow statistics are enabled on {device}, but NAT acceleration is turned off, so per-application data is not being recorded.

NAT acceleration and QoS/SQM (Smart Queue Management) cannot run at the same time on this router. To collect per-application statistics, disable QoS/SQM on the router's admin page. To keep QoS/SQM, turn off the Flow statistics switch to dismiss this notice.

tailscale_reauth_required.title: Tailscale needs re-authentication
tailscale_reauth_required.description:
Tailscale on {device} has dropped out of your tailnet and needs to be signed in again. This can happen when the Tailscale switch is toggled, because the router re-runs `tailscale up --reset`.

Open the router's Tailscale page (or the Tailscale status sensor's `auth_url` attribute) and sign in to reconnect. This notice clears automatically once the router is connected.

router_mode.title: Router is not in router mode
router_mode.description:
{device} is currently in {mode} mode. Tailscale and VPN features require the router to be in router mode and are unavailable in the current mode.

Switch the device to router mode on its admin page to use these features, or ignore this notice if you intend to run it in {mode} mode.
